title: AI-Powered Crop Monitoring Doubles Yields for Small-Scale Farmers
description: Satellite imagery combined with machine-learning models is enabling smallholder farmers to detect nutrient deficiencies and pest infestations weeks before visible symptoms appear, leading to reported yield increases of up to 110 %.
author: Priya Sharma

title: Government Launches ₹5,000 Cr Organic Farming Mission
description: The Ministry of Agriculture announced a new five-year mission to convert 10 million hectares to certified organic production, providing free soil-testing kits, bio-inputs, and premium market linkages to participating farmers.
author: Rahul Deshmukh

title: Drip Irrigation Adoption Reaches Record High in Semi-Arid Regions
description: Water-efficient drip and sprinkler systems now cover over 15 million hectares nationally, cutting water use by 40 % while improving crop quality and reducing fertiliser run-off in drought-prone districts.
author: Ananya Reddy

title: New Wheat Variety Resistant to Yellow Rust Released for Rabi 2026
description: ICAR has released HD-3386, a high-yielding wheat variety with built-in resistance to all prevailing races of yellow (stripe) rust, expected to boost productivity in northern plains by 8–12 %.
author: Dr. Vikram Singh

title: Tomato Prices Surge 60 % on Unseasonal Rains in Maharashtra
description: Unexpected heavy rainfall during the harvest window has damaged standing tomato crops across Nashik and Pune divisions, pushing wholesale prices to ₹80/kg and prompting the government to consider import relaxations.
author: Suresh Patil

title: Drone Spraying Gets Regulatory Green Light for 30 More Crops
description: The Central Insecticides Board has approved drone-based pesticide application for an additional 30 crop-pest combinations, making precision spraying accessible and reducing farmer exposure to harmful chemicals.
author: Kavita Nair

title: Community Seed Banks Preserve 200+ Heritage Crop Varieties
description: Farmer-led seed banks across 12 states are safeguarding indigenous rice, millet, and vegetable landraces that offer superior climate resilience compared to modern hybrids, with government recognition under the PPV&FR Act.
author: Meena Kumari

title: Soil Health Card Scheme 2.0 Introduces Real-Time NPK Sensors
description: The revamped Soil Health Card programme will distribute IoT-enabled soil sensors to 5 million farms, allowing real-time nitrogen, phosphorus, and potassium monitoring through a mobile app linked to fertiliser recommendations.
author: Arun Joshi
